diagrams.title: Cloud Computing Service Models Stack
diagrams.description: Shows the three service models (IaaS, PaaS, SaaS) as stacked layers with management responsibilities
elements: Hardware (Physical servers, storage, networking) at bottom
IaaS: Provider manages up to virtualization; User manages OS, apps, data
PaaS: Provider manages up to OS; User manages apps and data
SaaS: Provider manages everything; User only accesses application
User responsibility increases from bottom to top
diagrams.purpose: Helps visualize what is managed by provider vs user in each service model and understand the shared responsibility model

diagrams.title: Traditional vs Cloud Cost Comparison
diagrams.description: Graph comparing Capital Expenditure vs Operational Expenditure models over time
elements: Traditional Computing: High initial CapEx spike for hardware/datacenter, gradual maintenance costs, total cost remains high
Cloud Computing: Minimal initial cost, small incremental OpEx based on usage, lower total cost curve
Breakeven point where cloud becomes more cost-effective
diagrams.purpose: Visualize how cloud computing shifts from large upfront investments to pay-as-you-go model reducing financial burden

diagrams.title: Cloud Deployment Models
diagrams.description: Four deployment models showing different cloud infrastructure arrangements
elements: Public Cloud: Multiple organizations share provider's infrastructure
Private Cloud: Single organization with dedicated infrastructure
Hybrid Cloud: Public cloud + Private cloud connected together
Community Cloud: Shared by multiple organizations with common requirements
diagrams.purpose: Understand different ways organizations can deploy cloud resources based on their needs for control, security, and cost

diagrams.title: Elasticity in Cloud Computing
diagrams.description: Shows how cloud resources automatically scale with demand
elements: Demand line shows peaks and valleys throughout day
Resource allocation line follows demand curve automatically
Shows scaling up during peak (9am-5pm), scaling down at night
diagrams.purpose: Demonstrate how rapid elasticity automatically provisions and de-provisions resources matching demand in real-time
